news.summary: Liberland, a self-declared blockchain country, is striving for progressive governance despite encountering obstacles such as unverified assertions, absence of acknowledgement, and financial uncertainty. With an area of approximately 7 square kilometers and a population of around 1000, the country continues to navigate through these challenges on its path towards establishing itself as a recognized entity.
news.headline: Liberland, a blockchain nation, faces challenges like unverified claims and economic instability despite its innovative governance.

news.summary: ShareRing will be conducting a pilot program for its digital identity solution with students from various schools. This initiative is part of a federal effort to enhance online safety.
news.headline: ShareRing will test blockchain ID for age verification with Australian students to enhance online safety in a federal project.

news.summary: On Wednesday, the yield on U.S. Treasuries increased as investors opted to sell off government bonds, typically seen as a safe investment. In 2022, a similar prolonged increase in bond yields in Britain resulted in the resignation of the prime minister. This trend indicates a shift in investor confidence and market sentiment.
news.headline: U.S. bond yields rose as investors sold government bonds, reminiscent of a trend in the UK that led to a PM's downfall.

news.summary: Ukraine's financial regulator is proposing to tax certain crypto transactions at a rate of up to 23% as personal income, with an 18% tax and 5% military levy. Excluded from the tax are crypto-to-crypto transactions and stablecoins. The proposed framework, released on April 8 by the National Securities and Stock Market Commission, aims to provide lawmakers with an informed resolution by considering the advantages and disadvantages of each suggestion. The tax will apply when crypto is cashed out for fiat currency or exchanged for goods/services, aligning Ukraine with European countries like Austria and France, as well as crypto-friendly jurisdictions like Singapore, while also exempting stablecoins backed by foreign currencies. Additionally, the framework outlines taxation options for mining, staking, hard forks, and airdrops, suggesting exemptions for small investors and possibilities for donations, family transfers, and long-term holders. Last year, Ukraine's president signed a law establishing a regulated crypto market, with a draft bill to legalize cryptocurrencies currently under review.
news.headline: Ukraine considers 23% tax on crypto income, with exemptions for stablecoins and certain transactions. Framework released on April 8th.

news.summary: The recent economic instability, highlighted by a sharp increase in government bond yields, led to President Trump changing his stance on imposing high tariffs.
news.headline: Trump changed stance on tariffs as economic turmoil, especially sharp increase in bond yields, led to reversal.

news.summary: Leading cryptocurrencies experienced positive growth overnight following the confirmation of pro-cryptocurrency Paul Atkins as the new SEC Chairman. As of 8:30 p.m. ET, Bitcoin had surged by 7.60% to $82,319.38, Ethereum by 11.98% to $1,645.63, and Dogecoin by 10.69% to $0.1579. Bitcoin passed $82,000 and Ethereum crossed $1,600, leading to broader market gains and offsetting losses from the previous week's 'Liberation Day' actions. Over $370 million in short positions were liquidated in the last 24 hours, with $194 million from Bitcoin alone. The market shift followed President Trump's tariff pause announcement and the confirmation of Atkins, who focused on establishing a regulatory framework for digital assets. Atkins' appointment signals a positive outlook for cryptocurrency markets.
news.headline: Cryptocurrency prices surged with Bitcoin at $82,319.38 (+7.60%), Ethereum at $1,645.63 (+11.98%), and Dogecoin at $0.1579 (+10.69%) due to Trump tariff pause and Paul Atkins SEC confirmation. BTC expected to reach $87,000. Over $370 million in short positions wiped out. President Trump announced a 90-day tariff pause, and Atkins prioritizes digital asset regulation.

news.summary: In a recent announcement, AppsFlyer has collaborated with NAVER to integrate AppsFlyer Audiences with the platform in South Korea. This partnership aims to bolster audience targeting capabilities on NAVER, a popular search engine and portal. By leveraging AppsFlyer’s predefined audience segments, advertisers can enhance the precision of their marketing campaigns on the platform. The integration empowers advertisers to create detailed user cohorts, as well as evaluate and improve campaign effectiveness. This strategic move is expected to optimize marketing efforts on NAVER, maximizing impact and reaching a broader audience.
news.headline: AppsFlyer and NAVER integrate Audiences for precise marketing targeting, improving campaign impact.

news.summary: A new automated inspection device has been introduced by Fujitsu Limited, Sonofai Inc., Ishida Tec Co., Ltd., and Tokai University in Tokyo on April 9, 2025. This device aims to ensure consistent quality and maximize value through non-destructive inspection. By utilizing this technology, businesses can enhance their production processes and achieve higher levels of efficiency.
news.headline: Sonofai, Fujitsu, Ishida Tec, and Tokai University launch new automated inspection device for frozen tuna.

news.summary: A recently opened facility has improved supply chain security and shortened lead times while aiding in customers' expansion. It is the first in Asia to feature cutting-edge air knife coating technology and to manufacture both top and bottom substrates for medical device packaging. The facility, located in Zurich, was inaugurated on April 9, 2025.
news.headline: Amcor launches cutting-edge facility in Asia to improve healthcare packaging access, reducing lead times. #innovation

news.summary: On April 9, 2025, Harbour BioMed revealed its latest news in Cambridge, Mass., Rotterdam, Netherlands, and Shanghai. The company, listed as HKEX: 02142, is a prominent biopharmaceutical entity dedicated to uncovering and advancing innovative antibody treatments in the fields of immunology and oncology. This announcement underscores their commitment to leading advancements in the global healthcare sector.
news.headline: Harbour BioMed appoints Youchen Chen as CFO. Global biopharmaceutical company specializing in antibody therapeutics.

news.summary: Sayona Mining Limited, a publicly traded company with the ticker symbols ASX:SYA and OTCQB:SYAXF, has issued an update regarding its potential transaction with Piedmont Lithium Inc. The details of the transaction, including the financial figures and terms, have not been disclosed in the provided information.
news.headline: Sayona Mining Limited will be renamed Elevra Lithium and announces board nominees after proposing deal with Piedmont Lithium Inc. ASX:SYA; OTCQB:SYAXF.

news.summary: OpenSea, a leading online marketplace known as a digital bazaar, is advocating for regulatory entities to establish distinct boundaries between NFT markets, exchanges, and brokers. This platform, recognized for its wide array of NFT offerings, seeks to ensure clarity and transparency within the industry.
news.headline: OpenSea seeks SEC guidance on NFT rules to distinguish marketplaces, exchanges, and brokers in digital space.

news.summary: As per the latest report, the authorities have disclosed that the total number of confirmed COVID-19 cases has reached 10,000, with 500 new cases reported in the last 24 hours. Additionally, there have been 20 new fatalities, increasing the total number of deaths to 250. In response to this surge, the government is implementing stricter measures to curb the spread of the virus, including mandatory mask-wearing in public spaces and restrictions on large gatherings. The healthcare system is under immense pressure, with 80% of hospital beds currently occupied. Officials urge the public to adhere to guidelines to prevent further transmission of the virus.
news.headline: OpenAI urges to stop Musk's 'relentless' attacks, citing 5 billion instances created daily.

news.summary: The Automotive Steering System market is projected to grow from USD 35.3 billion in 2022 to USD 39.5 billion by 2027, with a CAGR of 2.3% over the forecast period. The rapid adoption of Electric Vehicles (EVs) worldwide is driving demand for Electrical Power Steering and Steer-by-Wire systems. Integration of Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS) with EPS has led to new steering features that customers widely accept. As more commercial EVs enter the market, the demand for EPS in those vehicles will rise. Steer-by-Wire technology is seen as the future of steering systems enabling better autonomous mobility. Key players include JTEKT Corporation, Nexteer, Robert Bosch, Hyundai Mobis, and NSK Ltd. Drivers in the market include the demand for Electrically assisted Hydraulic Power Steering in commercial vehicles, while challenges include low customer confidence in the adoption of steer-by-wire technology. The Asia Pacific region dominates the global market, with Electronic components seeing the fastest growth due to EPS adoption. EPS is becoming standard in passenger cars, helping reduce emissions, increase fuel efficiency, and improve maneuverability. LCVs in North American and European regions are also adopting EPS due to safety and environmental regulations.
news.headline: Automotive Steering System Market to reach $39.5B by 2027, growing at 2.3% CAGR globally, driven by EV adoption and EPS technology advancements.

news.summary: A company based in Century City, California, known as Ateliere Creative Technologies, has announced that through its subsidiary Ateliere Creative Technologies CM, AB, discussions are underway with key shareholders of Codemill AB (publ) (“Codemill”) regarding a possible extension of the deadline for completing the tender offer linked to Ateliere's intended purchase of Codemill. This decision to prolong the timeline is a result of the current worldwide financial uncertainty and trade fluctuations impacting markets.
news.headline: Ateliere requests extension for Codemill tender offer deadline due to global trade uncertainty.

news.summary: During a recent statement, Kevin O'Leary, known for his Shark Tank role, criticized China for unfair trade practices, such as intellectual property theft from U.S. companies. O'Leary noted China's failure to honor commitments made upon joining the World Trade Organization. He disapproved of the U.S.'s response and called for immediate action, advocating for a 400% tariff on Chinese imports. Despite the Trump administration's 90-day tariff pause globally, China's tariffs will surge to 125%. O'Leary stressed that fairness, not politics, should govern trade relations. These remarks arrive amidst escalating trade tensions and notable U.S. policy changes against China. It's time to hold China accountable and prevent them from continuing unfair practices.
news.headline: Kevin O'Leary criticizes Trump's 125% China tariffs, calling for 400% instead. He denounces unfair practices.
